purpose: Defines the `!dispatch [dashboardId] {task_id | --ready}` command for manual task dispatch. Two modes: single-task dispatch (validates task exists, has no progress or pending status, all deps satisfied; reads plan.json for context; dispatches with full prompt template) and `--ready` mode (scans all agents and dispatches every task whose deps are satisfied). Produces logs.json dispatch entries but does NOT write progress files (workers create their own).
gotchas: Master MUST NOT write a progress file when dispatching — the worker creates its own when it starts
Worker prompt MUST include {dashboardId}, {tracker_root}, AND {project_root} so the worker writes progress to the right dashboard and does code work in the right project
Validation rejects tasks with status other than pending — failed tasks must use !retry, completed tasks should not be re-dispatched
The simpler !dispatch --ready uses basic prompt construction — for production-quality prompts with conventions and upstream injection, use !eager_dispatch instead
patterns: 9-step single-task procedure: parse → read init → read progress → validate (existence, status, deps) → read plan → resolve project_root → dispatch with prompt → log → confirm
--ready mode replays steps 1-2 then iterates through all pending agents checking dep satisfaction
Worker prompt skeleton must always include three paths: PROJECT ROOT, TRACKER ROOT, and the progress file write path
conventions: Two parallel sub-sections for the two modes
Inline note callout about not writing progress files
References p_track.md Step 14 for the prompt template (avoids duplication)
